extraction.one_line_summary: VLASOV Aleksandr overcame 18-second deficit to DENNIS Rohan with dominant 33:40 climbing TT, winning by 30+ seconds over GESCHKE.
tactical_patterns: VLASOV's 30-second margin over GESCHKE on 11.3km climbing TT reveals GC contenders need TT-specific climbing skills for hilly stage races.
DENNIS faded dramatically after third-fastest intermediate split, suggesting TT specialists struggle to maintain power on extended 7.4% climbs.
KRUIJSWIJK rode aggressively for teammate DENNIS despite no GC chance, posting 17-second intermediate split advantage over eventual winner.

rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: positive: First World Tour win came via crushing climbing TT performance, suggesting untapped potential in mountain stages with TT elements.
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: negative: Lost 2+ minutes and GC lead after strong intermediate split indicates poor pacing strategy or fitness decline on sustained climbs.
rider_signals.GESCHKE Simon: positive: Jumped from ninth to podium with surprise second place, 33 seconds ahead of previous benchmark, revealing hidden climbing form.
rider_signals.MÄDER Gino: positive: Only 4 seconds behind GESCHKE for second overall as 19-year-old signals emerging GC threat in stage races with climbing TTs.
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: neutral: Held young rider jersey but fell off podium, showing age-appropriate ceiling against elite climbing TT specialists.
